description: Rules for handling images, icons, and static assets in {{PROJECT_NAME}}. Ensures performance, consistency, and scalability.

rules.title: Optimized Image Formats
rules.description: All images must be stored in optimized formats (WebP or AVIF preferred). JPG and PNG are only allowed if optimization tools cannot convert them without quality loss. Favicon must include multiple sizes (16x16, 32x32, 64x64) in `.ico` or `.png` format.
rules.rationale: Modern image formats significantly reduce file size while maintaining quality, improving page load times and Core Web Vitals scores.

problems: Large file size increases bandwidth usage

rules.title: Static Assets Directory Structure
rules.description: All static assets must be stored under `/public/assets/` with clear subfolders: Images → `/public/assets/images/`, Icons → `/public/assets/icons/`, Logos → `/public/assets/logos/`, Fonts → `/public/assets/fonts/`.
rules.rationale: Consistent directory structure improves maintainability, makes asset discovery easier, and ensures proper caching and build optimization.

badExample.code:
// ❌ BAD: Scattered assets
src/
  components/
    Header/
      logo.png
      icon.svg
  pages/
    Home/
      banner.jpg
  utils/
    font.ttf
problems: No discoverability - assets scattered across codebase

goodExample.code:
// ✅ GOOD: Organized structure
public/
  assets/
    images/
      banners/
        home-banner.webp
    icons/
      menu.svg
      close.svg
    logos/
      primary.svg
    fonts/
      inter.woff2
benefits: Single source of truth for all assets

rules.title: SVG Usage Policy
rules.description: SVGs must be used whenever possible for icons and vector graphics. Inline SVGs if they need to be dynamic (e.g., theming, animations). Static SVGs go inside `/public/assets/icons/`. Do not use rasterized PNG versions of SVGs.
rules.rationale: SVGs are resolution-independent, smaller in file size for icons, and can be styled with CSS. Using PNG versions of SVGs defeats these benefits.

rules.title: Asset Loading & Performance
rules.description: Assets must be lazy-loaded or optimized for minimal performance impact. Use `loading='lazy'` for non-critical images. Use responsive images (`srcset`, `sizes`) when necessary. Never import images directly into TypeScript/React files unless absolutely necessary (use public folder references).
rules.rationale: Lazy loading defers off-screen image loading, improving initial page load. Responsive images serve appropriately sized images for each viewport, reducing unnecessary bandwidth usage.
